purpose: A/B test briefs for Phase C-6 — verify MEMORY_INJECT=true reduces self-heal triggers vs MEMORY_INJECT=false on the same brief.
instructions: See scripts/memory-ab-compare.ts header for full workflow. Pick a brief, run it twice into separate output dirs, then compare.
verdict_threshold: On 5+ briefs averaged: self-heal triggers down ≥20% with no >5% cost regression.

briefs.feature_brief: Build a single-page Pomodoro timer web app. Users can start / pause / reset a 25-minute focus timer and a 5-minute break timer. Display the remaining time in MM:SS format. Show a session counter that increments on each completed cycle. No backend, no auth, no database — all state in memory.

Build a Todo app with a Node/Express backend and React frontend. Backend exposes REST endpoints for list/create/toggle/delete tasks (in-memory store, no DB). Frontend has a single page that lists tasks, supports adding via input, toggling done, and deleting. No auth, no persistence.

Build a personal blog with PostgreSQL via Prisma. Posts have title, slug, body (markdown), publishedAt. Admin can CRUD posts via a simple form (no auth in v1). Public homepage lists posts; clicking opens detail page. Use Next.js (App Router) + Prisma + Postgres in docker-compose.

Task management web app. Users create tasks with title, description, status (todo/doing/done), priority (P0/P1/P2). Filter by status / priority. Assign tasks to fake users (no real auth). React frontend, Node backend, in-memory storage.

Build a JavaScript-fundamentals quiz with 10 multiple-choice questions hardcoded in the frontend. Show one question at a time, user picks an answer, advance to next. At the end show score / 10 with a Restart button.

preconditions: Mining + approve done: at least 3-5 patterns at score >= 0.3 OR manual:approved.
Output dirs cleaned: pick fresh paths each run so .ralph/ doesn't carry over.
recipe.baseline_run: MEMORY_ENABLED=true MEMORY_INJECT=false  npm run electron:dev  →  kickoff brief into  out-baseline-<briefId>/
recipe.treatment_run: MEMORY_ENABLED=true MEMORY_INJECT=true  npm run electron:dev  →  kickoff same brief into  out-treatment-<briefId>/
recipe.attribution_after: After treatment run, run `npm run memory:attribute -- --project=out-treatment-<briefId>` so success/failure outcomes feed back into pattern scores.
